PAIN_1_DESC: Heavy reliance on a single processor for Nitro subscriptions and Boost renewals, any outage or policy change instantly suspends recurring revenue.
PAIN_2_DESC: 150M+ monthly active users across 190+ countries, yet checkout still routes through centralized, US-first acquiring, costly and failure-prone cross-border.
PAIN_3_DESC: Thin local payment coverage outside cards, missing UPI in India, Pix in Brazil, wallets across APAC, directly capping global Nitro conversion.
PAIN_4_DESC: No smart routing or cascade retries across acquirers, so recurring-subscription declines fall straight to involuntary churn instead of being recovered.
PAIN_5_DESC: Interchange, cross-border and FX fees are negotiated per processor with no competitive leverage, leaving material margin on the table at Discord's volume.
CAPABILITY_1_DESC: Per-transaction decisioning across every acquirer, lifting Discord's auth rate on Nitro and Boost renewals without a single engineering sprint.
CAPABILITY_2_DESC: Automatic cascade across processors rescues declined subscriptions in real time, turning involuntary churn into recovered revenue.
CAPABILITY_3_DESC: 1,000+ payment methods, wallets and local rails, UPI, Pix, iDEAL, Konbini, GrabPay, live through one integration, unlocking Discord's global conversion.
CAPABILITY_4_DESC: One reconciliation, one analytics layer, one contract surface across every PSP and market, replacing a fragmented ops mesh with a single control plane.
